name: Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA)
what_it_means: API fails to enforce permissions on actions/endpoints, letting basic users invoke admin or restricted functions.
animated_example: Pressing the elevator’s ‘staff only’ button and it still takes you to the restricted floor.
quick_checks: Server-side authorization on every action; admin routes isolated
HTTP method ↔ privilege mapping defined and enforced
No security-by-UI; backend blocks hidden/maintenance functions
Comprehensive allow/deny policies with least privilege
how_to_test: Use disallowed methods (DELETE where not permitted)
expected_outcomes: Privileged attempts logged with actor and scope
minimal_example: Normal user → POST /admin/rebuildIndex → 403; admin with MFA → 200
